name: Together AI
description: Gateway/host for open-weight models with an OpenAI-compatible API and fine-tuning

provider.comment: Operated by Together AI (Together Computer, Inc.), an inference/fine-tuning cloud for open-weight and proprietary models.

category.comment: A hosting gateway serving 200+ open-weight and proprietary models from many vendors behind one OpenAI-compatible API with unified billing; not a hyperscaler cloud platform and not a first-party model trainer.

api-launched.comment: Could not pin a primary-source month for the developer inference API this pass. Together AI was founded in 2022 and its hosted serverless inference API/playground became broadly available during 2023; exact GA month not confirmed from a fetched source (checked 2026-07).

status.comment: Developer API is generally available with published commercial pricing across serverless, dedicated, and batch.

api-docs.comment: Official Together AI developer documentation and API reference.

auth-methods.comment: Requests authenticated with a bearer API key (Authorization header).

self-serve-signup.comment: Fully self-serve — create an account and obtain an API key without sales contact; a positive prepaid credit balance (minimum $5 top-up) is required to make calls.

free-credits-trial.comment: No standing free-inference tier; Together's billing docs state the platform is fully prepaid with a $5 minimum credit purchase and the earlier signup credit was retired in July 2025. A separate startup accelerator grants $15k–$50k to qualifying companies (checked 2026-07).

regional-endpoints.comment: Serverless inference is primarily US-hosted; EU data centers are available for dedicated model deployment on Scale and Enterprise tiers (checked 2026-07).

data-residency-options.comment: Customers on Scale/Enterprise tiers can pin dedicated inference and storage to EU data centers — a contractual/config data-residency guarantee, not merely a shared endpoint.

model-breadth.comment: Serves model families from many vendors: Meta (Llama), DeepSeek, Alibaba (Qwen), Mistral, Moonshot (Kimi), MiniMax, Black Forest Labs (FLUX), and more.

open-weight-models.comment: Core focus is open-weight models — Llama, DeepSeek, Qwen, Mixtral, Kimi, etc. — served serverless and on dedicated endpoints.

model-version-pinning.comment: Models are invoked by explicit, immutable checkpoint identifiers (e.g. meta-llama/Llama-4-Scout-...), so a specific snapshot can be pinned.

custom-fine-tuned-deployment.comment: Supports LoRA/full fine-tuning, bring-your-own-model (BYOM), custom containers, and dedicated single-tenant endpoints to deploy and call your own/private models through the same API.

model-catalog-size.comment: Catalog spans 200+ models across chat, vision, image, audio, video, and embeddings (checked 2026-07).

streaming.comment: Token streaming (SSE) supported for incremental responses.

tool-function-calling.comment: Documented tool/function calling using the OpenAI-style tools parameter and tool_calls response; support is model-dependent.

parallel-tool-calls.comment: Documented parallel patterns — same function called multiple times, and multiple functions, in a single turn for concurrent execution.

structured-output.comment: Strongest guarantee is schema-constrained JSON via response_format (json_schema); looser JSON mode and regex/grammar-constrained decoding are also supported. Availability varies by model (checked 2026-07).

system-prompt.comment: Distinct system role supported via the OpenAI-compatible chat messages format.

image-input-vision.comment: Vision-capable models (e.g. Llama 4 Scout accepts up to 5 images, Qwen-VL, Kimi) accept image input.

audio-input.comment: Speech-to-text/transcription available via Whisper Large v3 and other audio models on the same API.

document-pdf-input.comment: No first-class native document/PDF input content type in the inference API documented; text must be extracted and passed in (checked 2026-07).

image-generation.comment: Image-generation endpoint offered on the same account (FLUX family, Nano Banana Pro, etc.).

text-to-speech-audio-output.comment: Text-to-speech / audio synthesis available on the same API (e.g. Cartesia Sonic, MiniMax Speech).

embeddings-endpoint.comment: Text-embeddings endpoint (e.g. multilingual-e5, BGE) plus a Rerank API for retrieval/semantic search on the same account.

prompt-caching.comment: Automatic prefix-based prompt caching for select serverless chat models; cached input tokens billed at a reduced rate with no toggle/parameter required (checked 2026-07).

batch-api.comment: Asynchronous Batch API at ~50% of real-time cost; up to 50,000 requests / 100 MB per batch file, best-effort 24-hour window, separate rate limits.

max-context-window.comment: Llama 4 Scout is served at a 327,680-token context on Together (page notes it will be increased toward 10M). Some other models (e.g. Llama 4 Maverick) are reported around 500k; largest served window varies and is rising (checked 2026-07).

extended-reasoning.comment: Reasoning models (e.g. DeepSeek-R1, Qwen reasoning variants) are served with visible thinking/reasoning output at the API level; capability is model-dependent.

built-in-web-search.comment: No first-party provider-hosted web-search/grounding tool exposed via the inference API; you wire your own search tool (checked 2026-07).

built-in-code-execution.comment: Together Code Interpreter (TCI) is a first-party sandboxed Python execution environment callable via REST API (session-based, $0.03/session).

mcp-support.comment: The inference API does not offer hosted/remote MCP connector support as part of a request. Together does publish its Code Interpreter as an MCP server (via Smithery) for MCP clients — the reverse direction — but that is not the API acting as an MCP client (checked 2026-07).

files-retrieval-api.comment: A Files API exists for uploading fine-tuning/batch data, but there is no managed retrieval/RAG service the model queries at inference time (checked 2026-07).

agents-assistants-api.comment: No higher-level stateful assistants/threads API with managed conversation state; the API exposes raw inference plus tools/code-interpreter primitives (checked 2026-07).

openai-compatible-api.comment: Exposes an OpenAI-Chat-Completions-compatible endpoint (base https://api.together.xyz/v1) usable with the OpenAI SDK via a base-URL swap.

official-sdks.comment: First-party Python and TypeScript/JavaScript SDKs; the OpenAI SDKs also work against the compatible endpoint.

native-api-style.comment: Primary request/response shape follows the OpenAI Chat Completions style.

webhooks-callbacks.comment: Batch job completion is retrieved by polling job status and downloading the output file; no push webhook/callback documented (checked 2026-07).

published-sla.comment: No public contractual uptime SLA figure found from a primary source this pass; enterprise-tier terms are negotiated (checked 2026-07).

rate-limit-model.comment: Serverless rate limits scale with account tier/usage; dedicated endpoints and Provisioned Throughput (PTUs) provide reserved capacity (checked 2026-07).

provider-fallback-routing.comment: Together hosts and serves its own model deployments; no automatic cross-provider failover/policy routing like a pure aggregator (checked 2026-07).

status-page.comment: Public status page exists at status.together.ai (tracking Inference, serverless endpoints, fine-tuning, model APIs). Page not directly fetched this pass.

no-training-default.comment: Privacy policy states data collected is not used to train Together's models without explicit opt-in and consent.

zero-data-retention.comment: A Zero Data Retention (ZDR) mode is available but opt-in via Privacy & Security settings; by default prompts/responses are stored in history. ZDR applies only going forward from activation.

compliance-certifications.comment: SOC 2 Type 2 examination completed; HIPAA-aligned with BAAs available for healthcare. GDPR and ISO 27001 not confirmed from the fetched sources this pass.

enterprise-sso-access-control.comment: SSO via SAML and OIDC (Okta, Entra, Google Workspace, JumpCloud) with automated provisioning/offboarding on Scale and Enterprise tiers.

pricing-model.comment: Per-token serverless usage pricing plus reserved capacity via dedicated endpoints and Provisioned Throughput (PTUs); GPU clusters priced per GPU-hour.

unified-billing.comment: One Together account/bill covers all reachable models and services.

cache-batch-discount.comment: Batch API at ~50% of real-time cost; prompt-cache reads billed at a reduced rate (roughly 5–10x cheaper input on select models). The two discounts stack.

cheapest-entry.comment: Lowest-friction paid start is self-serve pay-as-you-go per-token pricing (requires a $5 minimum prepaid top-up; no free tier).
